meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no project ideas (Advanced Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no project ideas. A Advanced-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Claude Code Skills Setup to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with project paralysis. Learn how to use Claude Code and Antigravity to architect, build, and deploy your first high-impact AI application.
content.answer_block: Overcoming project paralysis requires shifting from passive learning to active architecture. By leveraging Claude Code for rapid iterative development and Antigravity for structured scaffolding, you transform abstract ideas into production-ready code. This workflow forces technical rigor, ensures modular design, and builds a portfolio that commands immediate hiring attention.
content.intro:
The screen glows with the cursor’s rhythmic pulse—a blinking taunt against a blank IDE. You know the syntax, you’ve memorized the docs, yet the 'perfect' project remains an elusive ghost. This isn't a lack of talent; it is a failure of the feedback loop. When you lack a clear architectural roadmap, you lose the war before the first commit.

We are bridging that gap today. By combining the autonomous architectural power of Claude Code with the rapid prototyping capabilities of Antigravity, we strip away the friction of setup. You aren't just writing code; you are building an engine for rapid innovation that turns 'I don't know what to build' into 'Here is my production-ready MVP.'

steps.title: Define the Architectural Scope
steps.description: Before writing code, use Antigravity to map out your project constraints, ensuring your idea is scope-appropriate for a student portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Software Architect. Generate a modular, high-impact project roadmap for a student. Focus on a specific pain point in productivity, include a tech stack (Node.js/TypeScript), and define the core MVP features.

steps.title: Initialize the Scaffolding
steps.description: Establish the project foundation using Claude Code to ensure best practices in file structure and dependency management from the first line.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a file structure schema for a scalable SaaS MVP. Include folders for services, models, controllers, and tests. Output as a CLI-friendly directory tree.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create the directory structure based on the provided schema'

steps.title: Implement Core Business Logic
steps.description: Leverage Claude Code to write your primary utility functions, focusing on clean, testable, and documented code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust, typed service function that handles user authentication and data validation using Zod and standard API response patterns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'implement the auth service with Zod validation as specified'

steps.title: Automate Integration Testing
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate unit and integration tests, ensuring your logic holds up under stress before you move to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of Jest tests for the auth service. Focus on edge cases like malformed payloads and unauthorized access attempts.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'run and fix all identified issues in the auth service tests'

steps.title: Final Deployment Readiness
steps.description: Prepare the project for deployment by configuring environment variables and build scripts for CI/CD.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a production-ready Dockerfile and a GitHub Actions workflow file that runs linting, testing, and a build step.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a production-ready Dockerfile and CI workflow'

pro_tips: Always use the --architect-mode flag in Claude Code to force the AI to explain its technical trade-offs before generating code.
Treat Antigravity prompts as 'system messages'—the more technical the constraints (e.g., 'use O(n) complexity'), the better the output.
Use Claude Code's 'review' command on your own legacy code to instantly identify technical debt you didn't know you had.
Never accept the first code block; use the 'refine' command to ask for alternative architectural patterns like Event-Driven or Micro-frontend.
Keep your context window clean by creating separate 'feature-spec.md' files for Claude Code to reference during long sessions.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is running, deploy it to Vercel or Railway and document your architectural decisions in a README.md to demonstrate your engineering process to potential employers.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the exact workflow used by top-tier engineers to ship products from zero to one. The gap between your current knowledge and a professional portfolio is simply consistent, guided execution. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
